replies.author: @achillesjones70
replies.text: This video , Jeremy, makes it full circle. Over a decade ago,  after I watched TLJ in the cinema, I had a strange feeling, that I couldn't fully express. I went online and found Geeks + Gamers, where a dude with a  cap exactly expressed with the right words and intensity, what he thought about TLJ. And I thought, yes, yes that's what I felt, too, that's what I thought, too. We wanted to like Star Wars, but it wasn't possible....In fact, those who made the movie didn't know Star Wars. Let's see if a new era beggins.....Keep posting videos by saying: " What's up Geeks & Gamers, my name is Jeremy, coming to you with another videooooo....."

replies.text: @Taxguy99 Exactly my feelings. I already knew after the "Bart makes a phone prank scene" in the first minutes, this wasn't a good movie, but I was still not prepared for the unmigitated desaster that followed. After the end titles had ended, I was still sitting there, stunned about what I had seen and drove home with a feeling like after a concussion. This feeling didn't go away for another full day. And videos like G&G with Jeremy etc. showed me, I wasn't alone, I wasn't a minority, every real SW fan felt the same.

text:
